original_image_tags: A line graph titled "Atlas IIAS" plotting "Percent Impacts in 10-nm Interval" (y-axis, logarithmic scale from 0.1 to 100) against "Impact Range (nm)" (x-axis, linear scale from 0 to 350). The graph displays four curves:

elements.description: A line chart titled 'Figure 22. Impact-Range Distributions' plotting 'Percent Impacts in 10-nm Interval' (logarithmic Y-axis from 0.1 to 100) against 'Impact Range (nm)' (X-axis from 0 to 350). The chart contains a legend with four entries: 'Atlas IIAS', 'Theoretical', 'Breakup q-alpha = 5,000 deg-lb/ft²', 'Breakup q-alpha = 20,000 deg-lb/ft²', and 'No Breakup'. Four data lines plot the respective distributions, showing a steep rise at the 350 nm mark.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual elements of a line graph with the same title, axis labels, and scales, but Description B adds detail about the legend entries and a feature at the 350 nm mark that Description A omits, without contradiction.
